query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "evoarena: tracking memory evolution for robust llm agents in dynamic e" linguistics
rationale: This paper satisfies criterion (a) by investigating the same core mechanism of "memory evolution" and "long-term memory" in LLM agents, specifically focusing on the robustness and integrity of that memory against adversarial manipulation (poisoning), which is a critical aspect of tracking memory evolution in dynamic environments. It also meets criterion (b) by providing empirical benchmarks on the "robustness" of LLM agents' memory systems, a key dependent variable in the user's query about robu
